Class Surface

java.lang.Object
org.obsidium.graphics.Surface
public class Surface extends Object
A class that represents an image.
Since:
1.0

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Surface(int width, int height)
    Creates a surface with a set width an heigth.
    Surface(BufferedImage bufferedImage)
    Creates a surface from a buffered image.

  • Constructor Details

    • Surface

      public Surface(int width, int height)
      Creates a surface with a set width an heigth.
      Parameters:
      width - the width of the surface
      height - the height of the surface
      Since:
      1.0

    • Surface

      public Surface(BufferedImage bufferedImage)
      Creates a surface from a buffered image. ---advanced---

      This is mostly used by obsidium to create surfaces in the process of transformation, or loading a surface from an image. It automatically gets the width and height from the buffered image.

      Parameters:
      bufferedImage -
      Since:
      1.0

  • Method Details

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Returns the width of the image in pixels.
      Returns:
      the width
      Since:
      1.0

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Returns the height of the image in pixels.
      Returns:
      the height
      Since:
      1.0

    • getGraphics2D

      public Graphics2D getGraphics2D()
      Returns the drawing graphics used to draw things on this surface. ---advanced---
      Returns:
      Graphics2D
      Since:
      1.0

    • dispose

      public void dispose()
      Frees some memory of the surface.

      Disposes of the drawing graphics.

      Since:
      1.0

    • getBufferedImage

      public BufferedImage getBufferedImage()
      Returns the bufferd image of this surface. ---advanced---
      Returns:
      Graphics2D
      Since:
      1.0

    • blit

      public void blit(Surface other, int x, int y)
      Blits a surface to this surface.

      This method is designed to be used for 'pasting' a surface onto this surface. For example, if you want to draw pieces of a map to this surface to later blit the assembly to the window.

      Parameters:
      other - the surface that you want to blit to this surface
      x - the x coordinate that specifies where you want the image to be blitted
      y - the y coordinate that specifies where you want the image to be blitted
      Since:
      1.0
